title: Lunar Habitat Module Dispersion: Maximizing the Minimum Triangle Area
strategies: central ring baseline: place all n modules on a small regular n-gon of radius 0.55*inradius about the plot incentre (reproduces the checker baseline, ~0.1)
wider-ring search: sweep the concentric-ring radius and rotation phase, keeping the ring whose minimum triangle area is largest (still one ring, but pushed outward)

independent-point local search: seed from spread rings and a barycentric lattice, then repeatedly relocate a vertex of the current minimum-area triangle to a feasible spot that raises the global minimum triangle area, with seeded multi-restart and random kicks
boundary-aware / corner-exploiting layouts that push some modules toward the plot corners and edges to enlarge the smallest triangle formed by any three modules
